summary:
The available literature does not associate sternal skin conductance with hot flash assessment; instead, hot flashes are evaluated using symptom reporting or thermometric methods. Therefore, sternal skin conductance lacks sufficient clinical justification as a reliable proxy for measuring hot flashes. 

Final Answer: B
